description_long: This object is a blue metal guard rail commonly used in urban environments to control pedestrian or vehicle movement. It features a series of evenly spaced vertical bars connected by a rounded top rail, forming a sturdy linear barrier. The ends of the rail curve downward to form stable feet that rest on the ground, providing support and preventing tipping. The construction appears to be welded, with smooth joints and a uniform paint finish that resists weathering. The guard rail is designed to be both visually noticeable and physically robust, making it suitable for sidewalks, parks, or event crowd control. There are no visible reflective markings or additional attachments.
description: A blue metal guard rail with vertical bars and rounded ends, designed for urban crowd or traffic control. It has a smooth, painted finish and sturdy, curved feet for stability.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile and the curved end foot of the guard rail.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full length, vertical bars, and both curved feet.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the other curved end and the slim profile.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, showing the vertical bars and both feet from the opposite side.
